name: BTC Dominance Drop → Altcoin Season
description: When BTC dominance falls below ~48%, capital historically rotates into altcoins, producing broad altcoin rallies while BTC consolidates.
trigger_condition: BTC.d < 48%

name: Fed Rate Hike → Crypto Risk-Off
description: Federal Reserve interest-rate increases trigger risk-off sentiment; investors rotate out of speculative assets including crypto into USD and Treasuries.
trigger_condition: FOMC rate increase announced or CPI > expectations

name: Stablecoin De-peg → Contagion Selloff
description: A major stablecoin (USDT, USDC, DAI) trading significantly off peg triggers panic selling and liquidity flight across crypto markets.
trigger_condition: stablecoin price < $0.97 or > $1.03 for 15+ minutes

name: BTC Halving → Supply Shock Bull Cycle
description: Every ~4 years BTC block rewards halve, reducing miner-driven sell pressure. Historically preceded a 12–18 month bull cycle 3–6 months post-event.
trigger_condition: BTC block reward halving event

name: Exchange Inflow Spike → Selling Pressure
description: Large on-chain inflows to centralised exchanges signal holders preparing to sell, often preceding short-term price declines.
trigger_condition: 7-day exchange net inflow > 2 standard deviations above mean

name: Exchange Outflow Surge → Accumulation Signal
description: Sustained exchange outflows indicate holders withdrawing to cold storage (long-term conviction), reducing liquid supply and supporting prices.
trigger_condition: 7-day exchange net outflow > 2 standard deviations above mean

name: Government Ban / Exchange Crackdown → Sharp Selloff
description: Announcements of crypto bans, exchange shutdowns, or severe regulatory actions cause immediate sharp price declines and elevated volatility.
trigger_condition: Major government or SEC enforcement action announced

name: Major Institutional Buy / ETF Inflow → Sustained Rally
description: Announcement of a major institution (BlackRock, Fidelity, sovereign wealth fund) buying crypto or launching a product drives sustained demand.
trigger_condition: ETF filing, major institutional purchase, or sovereign wealth announcement

name: Miner Capitulation → Hash Rate Drop + Price Bottom Signal
description: When BTC price falls below miner break-even costs, distressed miners sell large reserves, accelerating declines but historically marking bottoms.
trigger_condition: Hash-ribbon death cross + sustained hash rate decline > 20%

name: High Leverage Buildup → Long/Short Squeeze
description: Extreme open interest relative to spot volume builds fragile leverage. A catalyst can trigger cascading liquidations amplifying directional moves 2–5×.
trigger_condition: Funding rate > 0.1%/8h OR OI/spot volume ratio > 3× average

name: Extreme Fear Index → Contrarian Buy Signal
description: When the Crypto Fear & Greed Index falls below 15 (Extreme Fear), markets are historically oversold and often produce strong 30-day reversals.
trigger_condition: Fear & Greed Index < 15

name: Extreme Greed Index → Correction Risk
description: When the Crypto Fear & Greed Index exceeds 85 (Extreme Greed), markets are historically overextended and near-term corrections of 10–30% become likely.
trigger_condition: Fear & Greed Index > 85

name: Ethereum Major Upgrade → ETH Outperformance
description: Ethereum protocol upgrades (e.g. EIP activations, Dencun, Prague) reduce friction or increase scalability, typically driving ETH outperformance vs BTC.
trigger_condition: Ethereum hard fork or major EIP activation

name: DeFi Protocol Hack → Sector Selloff
description: A large exploit (>$10 M) of a DeFi protocol triggers TVL flight, user panic, and a selloff in related tokens and sometimes the broader market.
trigger_condition: DeFi exploit > $10M reported

name: ETH Congestion → Alt-L1 / L2 Outperformance
description: High Ethereum gas prices drive users and capital to cheaper chains (Solana, AVAX, Arbitrum, Optimism), causing relative outperformance of alt-L1s.
trigger_condition: ETH gas price > 80 gwei sustained for 6+ hours

name: Recession Signals → Risk-Asset Selloff
description: Inverted yield curve, rising unemployment, or GDP contraction data trigger broad risk-off moves that correlate crypto with equity markets.
trigger_condition: US 2Y/10Y inversion deepens OR GDP estimate revised negative

name: US Dollar Weakness (DXY Drop) → Crypto Bull
description: A weakening US dollar historically correlates with crypto price appreciation as investors seek dollar-alternative stores of value.
trigger_condition: DXY 30-day change < -3%

name: Cross-Chain Bridge Exploit → Ecosystem Selloff
description: Bridge hacks destroy cross-chain liquidity and confidence. Assets bridged via the exploit often crash while the native chain sees collateral damage.
trigger_condition: Bridge exploit > $5M reported

name: BTC Spot ETF Inflow Surge → Demand Driven Rally
description: Consecutive days of large net inflows into Bitcoin spot ETFs (e.g. BlackRock IBIT) signal sustained institutional demand and drive price appreciation.
trigger_condition: ETF net inflows > $500M for 3+ consecutive days

name: Quarterly Options/Futures Expiry → Volatility Spike
description: Large options expiry events (especially quarterly CME, Deribit) cause pin-risk near max-pain price followed by post-expiry volatility.
trigger_condition: Quarterly futures/options expiry (last Friday of March, June, September, December)

name: Active Address Surge → Network Demand Signal
description: A sharp increase in daily active addresses indicates growing user demand for the network and often precedes price appreciation.
trigger_condition: 7-day average active addresses > 3-month average by 40%+

name: NFT Market Boom → ETH Gas Demand → ETH Price Lift
description: Surging NFT trading volumes on Ethereum drive up gas fees and ETH burn rate (EIP-1559), reducing circulating supply and supporting price.
trigger_condition: OpenSea / Blur 7-day volume > $500M

name: L2 Adoption Surge → ETH Sequencer Revenue Rise
description: Growth in L2 activity (Arbitrum, Optimism, Base) increases ETH fee burns and sequencer revenue, improving ETH fundamentals.
trigger_condition: L2 combined TVL increase > 30% in 30 days

name: Whale Accumulation → Price Support
description: On-chain data showing wallets > 1000 BTC increasing balances indicates smart-money accumulation, historically preceding price appreciation.
trigger_condition: Whale wallets (>1000 BTC) balance increase > 5% in 14 days

name: Staking Yield Compression → Capital Seeks Higher Yield
description: When ETH staking yields compress below 3%, yield-seeking capital migrates to higher-yield DeFi protocols or alt-L1 staking, pressuring ETH.
trigger_condition: ETH staking APR < 3% for 30+ days

name: Key Protocol Governance Vote → Token Price Reaction
description: High-stakes governance votes (fee switches, tokenomics changes, treasury deployments) create binary outcomes that sharply move the protocol's native token.
trigger_condition: Protocol governance vote passing or failing with > $100M TVL at stake

name: BTC Correlation Break → Alt-Independent Move
description: When BTC's 30-day correlation with S&P 500 falls below 0.2, crypto decouples from macro and alts can move independently on fundamentals.
trigger_condition: BTC-SPX 30d rolling correlation < 0.20

name: BTC Tests Realised Price → Historical Bottom Support
description: BTC touching its realised price (average on-chain cost basis) has historically been a strong accumulation zone and mean-reversion signal.
trigger_condition: BTC spot price within 5% of Realised Price

name: USDC Mass Redemptions → Liquidity Tightening
description: A large spike in USDC-to-USD redemptions reduces stablecoin liquidity available for crypto purchases, acting as a temporary demand suppressor.
trigger_condition: USDC supply decline > 5% in 7 days

name: Solana Ecosystem Catalyst → SOL Outperformance
description: Major Solana ecosystem launches (new DeFi protocol, NFT project, PayFi partnership) drive SOL demand as fees burn and users onboard.
trigger_condition: Solana major dApp launch or partnership with user-base > 1M
